steps.title: Test choice A with intercepts
steps.content: Use Desmos as an answer-choice checker. The graph in the question gives three fast targets: both lines share the $x$-intercept $(8,0)$, and the $y$-intercepts are positive, about $(0,3.2)$ and $(0,6.4)$. Graph choice A by itself, then compare it to those target points. Choice A's lines hit the $x$-axis at $(4,0)$ and $(6.4,0)$, so they do not share the required intercept $(8,0)$. Choice A is out.

steps.title: Test choice B with the same targets
steps.content: Clear A and graph only choice B. The same intercept check fails again: the two lines cross the $x$-axis at $(4,0)$ and $(6.4,0)$ instead of sharing $(8,0)$. They also slope upward from left to right, while the lines in the question slope downward. Choice B is out.

desmosGraphs.label: Choice B: wrong intercepts and slope

steps.title: Test choice C after the x-intercept works
steps.content: Clear B and graph only choice C. Now both lines do pass through $(8,0)$, so C survives the first check. But the $y$-intercepts are on the wrong side of the axis: $(0,-3.2)$ and $(0,-6.4)$ instead of positive $(0,3.2)$ and $(0,6.4)$. Choice C has the shared $x$-intercept but the wrong vertical placement, so C is out.

desmosGraphs.label: Choice C: right x-intercept, wrong y-intercepts

steps.title: Test choice D last
steps.content: Clear C and graph only choice D. The first line, $4x+10y=32$, passes through $(8,0)$ and $(0,3.2)$. The second line, $-8x-10y=-64$, passes through $(8,0)$ and $(0,6.4)$. Both lines slope downward, so D matches the shared $x$-intercept, both positive $y$-intercepts, and the direction of the graph.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: A and B miss the shared $x$-intercept. C has the correct shared $x$-intercept but the wrong side of the $x$-axis. D is the only pair with shared $x$-intercept $(8,0)$, positive $y$-intercepts $(0,3.2)$ and $(0,6.4)$, and downward slopes. Choice D is correct.
